last_completed_step: Frontend v3: removed admin-key requirement from UI -> dashboard is keyless trusted same-origin console with open /dashboard/api/* write endpoints (generate, config PUT, key create/list/revoke) sharing logic via app/services.py; public /plants & /admin APIs still key-protected (verified 401). Added realism 'texture' (already wired: deterministic cloud-flicker + wind-turbulence; wind block-to-block roughness ~4.6x with daily energy/caps preserved). Live tab self-heals (auto-generates today once if missing). Production safety valve DASHBOARD_CONSOLE_WRITE. 37 tests pass; ruff clean; all 5 tabs verified end-to-end in browser (Generate/Config/Weather/Keys) with no console errors.
next_action: DONE. Nothing required unless the user requests changes.
design_decisions: Site location not present in Book1 (4).xlsx (verified: no lat/long text/coordinates anywhere in the 35k-row sheet). Per spec, location is a configurable placeholder. Seed defaults to Jaisalmer, Rajasthan (26.9124, 70.9026, Asia/Kolkata) - strong solar+wind. Override via .env (PLANT_LAT/PLANT_LON/PLANT_TZ) or DB.
DB: SQLAlchemy via DATABASE_URL; SQLite zero-config local default, Postgres in docker-compose. Alembic migration (187b7cae8814) + create_all bootstrap fallback.

Open-Meteo: wind requested in m/s (wind_speed_unit=ms) for the power curve. minutely_15 used natively when present, else robust hourly->15min interpolation; ERA5 archive (hourly) flagged interpolated.

Single generation_block table holds the per-block hybrid record (solar+wind+total), queryable by data_mode; reprocess preserves history via is_current demotion + versioned rows. Documented as a justified deviation from 3 separate output tables.
Scheduler: APScheduler in-process (daily + live-refresh) and standalone runner for the docker scheduler service. Dashboard served by FastAPI at /dashboard with open read-only feeds (no secrets).
Capacities from workbook: Solar AC=160, DC=240 (1.5), Wind AC=135, PPA=140, 96 15-min blocks/day.

tasks.name: Scaffold, config, DB models, migrations, seed
